events.summary: @trq212 argues in a high-traction X article that agent outputs are shifting from Markdown summaries toward HTML artifacts users can inspect, share, and keep; the examples repo has multiple self-contained HTML outputs.
@omarsar0 connects the same shift to AI work products: HTML pages and small interactive artifacts can communicate long-horizon agent work better than a plain Markdown summary.

@pushmatrix shares Shopify Quick and Artifact: a separate internal hosting/sharing system for generated HTML apps and work products. It is related to the earlier HTML-output story, but it is its own project and product surface.

Vercel launched Vercel Drop, a separate platform feature for sharing and publishing generated web artifacts. It is its own product update and also adds another example to the shareable-artifact trend.
